name: Aim
pricing_notes: OSS self-hosted free; Aim Cloud (coming) will have commercial tier

auth.notes: Local file-based storage; optional remote server with basic auth

performance.notes: Local file I/O; Aim's columnar storage highly performant for metric queries; faster than MLflow for many runs

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source experiment tracker with focus on querying and exploring many runs. Custom columnar storage (not SQLite like MLflow) enabling fast cross-run metric queries via Python SDK. Beautiful UI. LLM evaluation tracking support (text, images, embeddings). REST API via Aim server. Integrates with HuggingFace Transformers, PyTorch Lightning, Keras.

gotchas: Smaller community than MLflow or W&B — fewer integrations
No managed cloud offering yet (self-hosted only)
REST API less documented than Python SDK
Breaking changes between major versions (2.x → 3.x migration required)
best_when: Teams wanting fast cross-run metric queries without cloud dependency
LLM evaluation tracking (text, image, embedding logging with context)
Teams who find MLflow UI slow for large-scale runs
Self-hosted experiment tracking with beautiful UI and no SaaS cost
avoid_when: Teams wanting enterprise SLA and cloud hosting
Largest integration ecosystem (MLflow or W&B better connected)
Collaboration across distributed teams (no cloud sync without Aim server)
summary: Aim is an open-source experiment tracker with a fast custom columnar storage optimized for querying across many runs. Beautiful UI, Python SDK, and REST API. LLM evaluation tracking support (text, images, embeddings). Self-hosted only. Faster than MLflow for cross-run metric queries. Best for self-hosted ML teams wanting fast metric exploration and LLM evaluation tracking without W&B/Comet costs.
